verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a type I transmembrane protein resident in the early secretory pathway (ER, ERGIC, Golgi). Its TM domain anchors it to endomembranes, and its luminal domains reside in the ER-Golgi lumen, not the extracellular space. It functions as an intracellular cargo receptor mediating vesicular transport between ER and Golgi. No evidence of surface-directed therapeutics, stress-induced ecto-presentation, cell-death externalization, tissue-restricted surface display, lysosomal exocytosis to the PM, dual PM residency, or stable post-translational surface anchoring. The protein's role in 'trafficking to the plasma membrane' refers to its cargo-handling function, not its own localization.
